title: An overview of ArctosR
text: order use functions ArctosR, request API key Arctos. See https://handbook.arctosdb.org/documentation/api.html instructions .

title: An overview of ArctosR
text: Arctos contains specimen records diverse types data associated (e.g., measurements, locality coordinates descriptions, tissue samples available, etc.). full description Arctos data visit website.

title: An overview of ArctosR
text: ArctosR abstracts interacting Arctos API via objects. objects can manipulated set using friendly functions tasks, created manipulated directly using builder functions.

previous_headings: Basics > Queries and responses in ArctosR
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: query ArctosR collection searches user package specific task. can : (1) simple search, one-search specimens species held museum; (2) search requires concatenation multiple responses, one requests records Arctos can provide single response; (3) complex search uses requests one set search terms, uses responses part another request.

previous_headings: Basics > Queries and responses in ArctosR
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: response object stores contents one response Arctos back ArctosR. response associated metadata, search terms time-stamp, content, table records matching search terms.

previous_headings: Basics > Queries and responses in ArctosR
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: Detailed metadata response also saved user’s query purposes documentation reproducibility. metadata saved along downloaded records JSON file.

title: An overview of ArctosR
text: Make sure load package:

title: An overview of ArctosR
text: ArctosR provides single function, get_records, search records Arctos database. order start building search, find possible query parameters can use search. can found get_query_parameters function, returns data frame query parameters used Arctos. names obj_name column used parameters get_records function. basic query, can use guid_prefix, whose description can found listing row query parameter data frame: also use scientific_name: Now set parameters use, can pass get_records function send request Arctos. return query, bundles search parameters returned data Arctos. simple search returned default (core) columns provided Arctos. lot information can requested Arctos, see example .
code: # You will have to request an API key from Arctos to get records, and pass it # to the `get_record_count` and `get_records` functions through the `api_key` # parameter. YOUR_API_KEY <- "11111111-2222-3333-4444444444444444"  # run the function and store results in an object query_params <- get_query_parameters()  # checking the data frame obtained (showing only 6 rows and 3 columns) query_params[1:6,1:3] #>             display                obj_name category #> 1     Verbatim Date           verbatim_date    event #> 2 Collecting Method       collecting_method    event #> 3 Collecting Source       collecting_source    event #> 4        Ended Date              ended_date    event #> 5  Event Attributes evtAttributeSearchTable    event #> 6           Habitat                 habitat    event # checking row 37 in the data frame query_params[37,1:5] #>       display    obj_name   category subcategory #> 37 Collection guid_prefix identifier       basic #>                                                                description #> 37 Collection responsible for the record. Turning this off will break most #>    forms. query_params[28,1:5] #>    display obj_name       category subcategory #> 28   Genus    genus identification  curatorial  #>                                               description #> 28 Genus as provided in collection's preferred Source(s).  query_params[23,1:5] #>    display obj_name       category subcategory  #> 23 Species  species identification  curatorial #>                                                            description #> 23 Species (binomial) as provided in collection's preferred Source(s). query <- get_records(guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm", scientific_name = "Canis lupus",                       api_key=YOUR_API_KEY)

what: Requesting other columns than the core set
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: default, columns returned Arctos category core. columns can listed follows: Additional columns can requested passing vector result parameters get_records function columns parameter like :
code: result_params <- get_result_parameters() result_params[result_params$category == 'core',1:2] #>                      display                      obj_name #> 1  GUID (DarwinCore Triplet)                          guid #> 7              Identified As               scientific_name #> 43          Asserted Country                       country #> 44   Asserted State/Province                    state_prov #> 51         Specific Locality                 spec_locality #> 57             Verbatim Date                 verbatim_date #> 69          Decimal Latitude                       dec_lat #> 70         Decimal Longitude                      dec_long #> 71      Coordinate Error (m) coordinateuncertaintyinmeters # making a list of additional columns to get (see get_query_parameters()) add_cols <- list("guid", "scientific_name", "relatedcatalogeditems", "collectors",                  "state_prov", "spec_locality", "dec_lat", "dec_long",                   "verbatim_date", "examined_for", "detected", "not_detected")  # getting records with additional columns query <- get_records(guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm", scientific_name = "Canis lupus",                       columns = add_cols, api_key=YOUR_API_KEY)

previous_headings: Making queries to Arctos > Requesting other columns than the core set
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: Certain result parameters (columns) Arctos entire tables associated single specimen record. instance, partdetail, links attributes part listed parts associated specimen. can requested just like result parameter. information complex columns obtained JSON format, can expanded data frames function expand_column. See example expanding columns section Expanding columns
code: # defining the columns to be obtained some_cols <- list("guid", "parts", "partdetail")  # performing the query query <- get_records(guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm", genus = "Canis",                       species = "lupus", columns = some_cols,                      api_key=YOUR_API_KEY)

title: An overview of ArctosR
text: default, get_records avoids requesting records query unless otherwise asked. passing parameter all_records = TRUE get_records, user can request ArctosR make multiple requests records given query downloaded.
code: get_record_count(guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm", scientific_name = "Canis lupus",                  api_key=YOUR_API_KEY) #> [1] 1694  query <- get_records(guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm", scientific_name = "Canis lupus",                       all_records = TRUE, api_key=YOUR_API_KEY)

what: Downloading and using data from Arctos
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: gone basic functionality ArctosR. can find example using package get, process, explore, save data Arctos.

previous_headings: Downloading and using data from Arctos
title: An overview of ArctosR
code: # a list of columns to download with the query my_cols <- list("guid", "scientific_name", "parts", "collectors", "state_prov",                  "spec_locality", "dec_lat", "dec_long", "verbatim_date",                 "partdetail")  # download records query <- get_records(guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm", scientific_name = "Canis lupus",                       columns = my_cols, api_key=YOUR_API_KEY)  # getting only the data frame of data msb_wolves <- response_data(query)

previous_headings: Downloading and using data from Arctos > Get and view data
title: An overview of ArctosR
text: can filter records presence absence certain data. example, find rodents MSB:Mamm collection Orthohantavirus, can use filter_by argument get_records. filter_by takes list record attributes attribute values. example, record attribute "detected", attribute value "Orthohantavirus".

title: An overview of ArctosR
Parasite search and mapping
text: First, can view parameters can use search Arctos: query parameters interested using : guid_prefix, genus, country. Next, can view list parameters can ask Arctos return calling: parameters interested returning : guid, scientific_name, dec_long, dec_lat, verbatim_date, parts, partdetail. parameter category. interested certain categories result parameter, can filter data.frame returned get_result_parameters() like :
code: # Request a list of all query parameters. query_params <- get_query_parameters()  # Explore all parameters. View(query_params) # Request a list of all result parameters. These are the names that can show up # as columns in a dataframe returned by ArctosR. result_params <- get_result_parameters()  # Explore all parameters. View(result_params) # Check only core and record parameters. result_params[result_params$category %in% c("core", "record"), 1:3]

title: Parasite search and mapping
text: Next, find number specimens matching query wanted perform. first three arguments give values query parameters queried get_query_parameters function. use filter_by parameter filter specimens attribute ‘examined ’ value ‘parasite’ indicates specimen undergone screening parasites. Attributes additional data record quantitative information record additional processing done record. helpful call first make sure aren’t asking many items Arctos. Next, download data, specify query, use columns parameter list result parameters want query. Finally, specify want download records. necessary Arctos paginates results, returning 100 time. Setting all_records = TRUE lets get_records repeatedly query Arctos receives records search. Arctos, table entries, partdetail tables. can expand tables data.frames using: object returned get_records contains data metadata request useful making requests based data returned first request. get data.frame response, use response_data pass response:
code: # Request just the number of records matching a query. count <- get_record_count(country = "Mongolia", genus = "Microtus",                           guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",                           filter_by=list("examined for"="parasite"),                           api_key=YOUR_API_KEY) # Request to download all available data matching a query (specific columns).  microtus <- get_records(country = "Mongolia", genus = "Microtus",                         guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",                         columns = list("guid", "scientific_name", "dec_long",                                         "dec_lat", "verbatim_date", "parts",                                         "partdetail"),                         filter_by=list("examined for"="parasite"),                         all_records = TRUE,                         api_key=YOUR_API_KEY) # Expand a column that contains complex information in JSON format expand_column(query = microtus, column_name = "partdetail") # Grab the dataframe of records from the response. microtus_df <- response_data(microtus)  # Filter out records which are missing latitude and longitude information microtus_df <- microtus_df[microtus_df$dec_lat != "" & microtus_df$dec_long != "", ]

title: Parasite search and mapping
text: Now, write custom function check partdetail entries specimen whether nematodes present specimen.
code: # Filter the data to keep only Microtus records in which nematodes were found ## Whole-word match for 'nematode' or 'nematodes' pattern <- "\\bnematodes?\\b"  ## A small function to check within data.frames in partdetail  has_nematode <- function(df) {   if (!is.data.frame(df) || is.null(df[["part_name"]])) {     return(FALSE)   } else {     return(any(grepl(pattern, df[["part_name"]], ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE)))   } }  ## Add column that is TRUE when a record has a nematode and FALSE otherwise microtus_df$nematode <- sapply(microtus_df$partdetail, has_nematode)  ## Subset of microtus_df with matches microtus_df_nematode <- microtus_df[microtus_df$nematode == TRUE, ] microtus_df_no_nematode <- microtus_df[microtus_df$nematode == FALSE, ]  ## Number of Microtus from Mongolia  nrow(microtus_df) #> [1] 886  ## Number of Microtus from Mongolia that had nematodes nrow(microtus_df_nematode) #> [1] 111  ## Number of Microtus from Mongolia that did not have nematodes nrow(microtus_df_no_nematode) #> [1] 775

title: Parasite search and mapping
text: can plot spatial distribution specimens without detected nematodes using ggplot2 maps packages. First, can simply create plot distribution specimens overMongolia.  Now really highlights ArctosR’s strengths; can use additional data Arctos provides plot Microtus Microtus without detected nematodes using filter established earlier.
code: # Initial plot of Microtus sampled in Mongolia  plot_map <- map_data("world", region=c("Mongolia"))  mongolia1 <- ggplot(plot_map, aes(long, lat)) +   geom_polygon(     aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray20",     linewidth = .2) +   geom_jitter(     data = microtus_df,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "Microtus", shape = "Microtus"),     alpha = .5, size = 1.0   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Microtus" = "red"),     labels = c("*Microtus*")   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Microtus" = 16),     labels = c("*Microtus*")   ) +   labs(     color = "Genus", shape = "Genus", x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   coord_sf(     crs = "+proj=lcc +lat_1=44 +lat_2=50 +lat_0=47 +lon_0=103.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs",     xlim = c(87, 120),      ylim = c(40, 52.5),     default_crs = sf::st_crs(4326),     expand = FALSE   ) +   annotation_scale(     location = "bl",     width_hint = 0.4   ) +   annotation_north_arrow(     location = "bl",     which_north = "true",      pad_x = unit(0.2, "in"),     pad_y = unit(0.3, "in"),     style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue"),     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1),     legend.position = "none"   )  ggsave(   filename = "figures/mongolia1.png",   plot = mongolia1,   width = 6.53,   height = 6.53,   dpi = 600,   create.dir = TRUE ) # install.packages("patchwork") library(patchwork)  # Plot of Microtus sampled in Mongolia filtered by nematode detected/not detected  plot_map <- map_data("world", region=c("Mongolia"))  mongolia2 <- ggplot(plot_map, aes(long, lat)) +   geom_polygon(     aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray20",     linewidth = .2   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = microtus_df_no_nematode,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "Microtus", shape = "Microtus"),     alpha = .5, size = 2.0   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = microtus_df_nematode,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "Microtus nematodes", shape = "Microtus nematodes"),     alpha = .5, size = 2.0   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("Microtus" = "red", "Microtus nematodes" = "black"),     labels = c("*Microtus*", "*Microtus* with nematodes")   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("Microtus" = 16, "Microtus nematodes" = 17),     labels = c("*Microtus*", "*Microtus* with nematodes")   ) +   labs(     color = "Genus", shape = "Genus", x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   coord_sf(     crs = "+proj=lcc +lat_1=44 +lat_2=50 +lat_0=47 +lon_0=103.5 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs",     xlim = c(87, 120),      ylim = c(40, 52.5),     default_crs = sf::st_crs(4326),     expand = FALSE   ) +   annotation_scale(     location = "br",     width_hint = 0.4   ) +   annotation_north_arrow(     location = "br",     which_north = "true",      pad_x = unit(0.2, "in"),     pad_y = unit(0.3, "in"),     style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue"),     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.25),     legend.position = c(0.0025, 0.0025),     legend.justification = c(0, 0),     legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),     legend.text = element_markdown(),     legend.title = element_blank()   )  ggsave(   filename = "figures/mongolia2.png",   plot = mongolia2,   width = 6.5,   height = 4,   dpi = 600,   create.dir = TRUE )

title: Species search and mapping
text: First, can view parameters can use search Arctos: query parameters interested using : guid_prefix, genus, species, state_prov. Next, can view list parameters can ask Arctos return calling: parameters interested returning : dec_lat dec_long.
code: # Request a list of all query parameters. query_params <- get_query_parameters()  # Explore all parameters. View(query_params) # Request a list of all result parameters. These are the names that can show up # as columns in a dataframe returned by ArctosR. result_params <- get_result_parameters()  # Explore all parameters. View(result_params)

title: Species search and mapping
text: Next can first ask count many records expect search return. count large can proceed request records. response can get data.frame using response_data function.
code: count <- get_record_count(guid_prefix="MVZ:Mamm",                            scientific_name="Tamias alpinus",                           state_prov="California",                           api_key=YOUR_API_KEY)  alpinus <- get_records(guid_prefix="MVZ:Mamm", scientific_name="Tamias alpinus",                        state_prov="California",                        columns=list("dec_lat", "dec_long"),                        all_records=TRUE, api_key=YOUR_API_KEY) alpinus_df <- response_data(alpinus)  # Filter out records which are missing latitude and longitude information alpinus_df <- alpinus_df[alpinus_df$dec_lat != "" & alpinus_df$dec_long != "", ]  nrow(alpinus_df) #> [1] 424

title: Species search and mapping
text: Now use dec_lat dec_long fields data.frame create plot: result :
code: # Plot of Tamias alpinus sampled in California and currently stored in the # Museum of Vertebrate Zoology's collection  plot_map <- map_data("state", region=c("California"))  plot <- ggplot(plot_map, aes(long, lat)) +   geom_polygon(     aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray20",     linewidth = .2   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = alpinus_df,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "T. alpinus", shape = "T. alpinus"),     alpha = .5, size = 2.0   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("T. alpinus" = "red"),     labels = c("*Tamias alpinus*")   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("T. alpinus" = 16),     labels = c("*Tamias alpinus*")   ) +   labs(     color = "Species", shape = "Species", x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   coord_sf(     crs = "+proj=lcc +lat_1=34 +lat_2=40.5 +lat_0=37.25 +lon_0=-119.25 +x_0=0 +y_0=0 +datum=WGS84 +units=m +no_defs",     xlim = c(-125.5, -113.5),     ylim = c(32, 42.5),     default_crs = st_crs(4326),     expand = FALSE   ) +   annotation_scale(     location = "bl",     width_hint = 0.4   ) +   annotation_north_arrow(     location = "bl",     which_north = "true",      pad_x = unit(0.2, "in"),     pad_y = unit(0.3, "in"),     style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue"),     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.25),     legend.position = c(0.998, 0.998),     legend.justification = c(1, 1),     legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),     legend.text = element_markdown(),     legend.title = element_blank()   )  ggsave(   filename = "figures/california.png",   plot = plot,   width = 4,   height = 4,   dpi = 600,   create.dir = TRUE )

title: Frozen tissue search
text: First, can view parameters can use search Arctos: query parameter interested using : scientific_name. Next, can view list parameters can ask Arctos return calling: parameters interested returning : dec_lat, dec_long, parts.
code: # Request a list of all query parameters. query_params <- get_query_parameters()  # Explore all parameters. View(query_params) # Request a list of all result parameters. These are the names that can show up # as columns in a dataframe returned by ArctosR. result_params <- get_result_parameters()  # Explore all parameters. View(result_params)

title: Frozen tissue search
code: cols <- list("dec_lat", "dec_long", "parts")  # Southern red-backed vole gapperi_query1 <- get_records(   scientific_name = "Clethrionomys gapperi",   columns = cols,   all_records = TRUE,   api_key = YOUR_API_KEY )  gapperi_query2 <- get_records(   scientific_name = "Myodes gapperi",   columns = cols,   all_records = TRUE,   api_key = YOUR_API_KEY )  # Northern red-backed vole rutilus_query1 <- get_records(   scientific_name = "Clethrionomys rutilus",   columns = cols,   all_records = TRUE,   api_key = YOUR_API_KEY )  rutilus_query2 <- get_records(   scientific_name = "Myodes rutilus",   columns = cols,   all_records = TRUE,   api_key = YOUR_API_KEY ) gapperi_df <- rbind(   response_data(gapperi_query1),   response_data(gapperi_query2) ) rutilus_df <- rbind(   response_data(rutilus_query1),   response_data(rutilus_query2) ) # Plot of overall species ranges  plot_map <- map_data("world", region=c("USA", "Canada"))  vole1 <- ggplot(plot_map, aes(long, lat)) +   geom_polygon(     aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray20",     linewidth = .2   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = gapperi_df,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "C. gapperi", shape = "C. gapperi"),     alpha = .5, size = 1.0   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = rutilus_df,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "C. rutilus", shape = "C. rutilus"),     alpha = .5, size = 1.0   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("C. gapperi" = "red", "C. rutilus" = "blue"),     labels = c("*Clethrionomys gapperi*", "*Clethrionomys rutilus*")   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("C. gapperi" = 16, "C. rutilus" = 17),     labels = c("*Clethrionomys gapperi*", "*Clethrionomys rutilus*")   ) +   labs(     # title = "Filtering *C. gapperi* and *C. rutilus* by tissue availability",     color = "Species", shape = "Species", x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   coord_sf(     crs = "+proj=lcc +lat_1=52 +lat_2=68 +lon_0=-134.5 +datum=WGS84",     xlim = c(-155, -114),      ylim = c(45, 65),     default_crs = sf::st_crs(4326),     expand = FALSE   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue"),     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1.25),     legend.position = c(0.0025, 0.0025),     legend.justification = c(0, 0),     legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),     legend.text = element_markdown(),     legend.title=element_blank()   )  ggsave(   filename = "figures/vole1.png",   plot = vole1,   width = 6.53,   height = 4.25,   dpi = 600,   create.dir = TRUE ) #> Warning: Removed 1197 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). #> Warning: Removed 1043 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). gapperi_df_tissue <- gapperi_df[   grepl("frozen", gapperi_df$parts, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE), ]  rutilus_df_tissue <- rutilus_df[   grepl("frozen", rutilus_df$parts, ignore.case = TRUE, perl = TRUE), ]  nrow(gapperi_df_tissue) #> [1] 3778 nrow(rutilus_df_tissue) #> [1] 7121 # Plot of overall species ranges, filtering by specimens with frozen tissue  vole2 <- ggplot(plot_map, aes(long, lat)) +   geom_polygon(     aes(group = group), fill = "white", color = "gray20",     linewidth = .2   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = gapperi_df_tissue,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "C. gapperi", shape = "C. gapperi"),     alpha = .5, size = 1.0   ) +   geom_jitter(     data = rutilus_df_tissue,     aes(x = as.numeric(dec_long), y = as.numeric(dec_lat),     color = "C. rutilus", shape = "C. rutilus"),     alpha = .5, size = 1.0   ) +   scale_color_manual(     values = c("C. gapperi" = "red", "C. rutilus" = "blue"),     labels = c("*Clethrionomys gapperi*", "*Clethrionomys rutilus*")   ) +   scale_shape_manual(     values = c("C. gapperi" = 16, "C. rutilus" = 17),     labels = c("*Clethrionomys gapperi*", "*Clethrionomys rutilus*")   ) +   labs(     color = "Species", shape = "Species", x = NULL, y = NULL   ) +   coord_sf(     crs = "+proj=lcc +lat_1=52 +lat_2=68 +lon_0=-134.5 +datum=WGS84",     xlim = c(-155, -114),      ylim = c(45, 65),     default_crs = sf::st_crs(4326),     expand = FALSE   ) +   annotation_scale(     location = "bl",     width_hint = 0.4   ) +   annotation_north_arrow(     location = "bl",     which_north = "true",      pad_x = unit(0.2, "in"),     pad_y = unit(0.3, "in"),     style = north_arrow_fancy_orienteering   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(     panel.background = element_rect(fill = "aliceblue"),     panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 1),     legend.position = "none"   )  ggsave(   filename = "figures/vole2.png",   plot = vole2,   width = 6.53,   height = 6.53,   dpi = 600,   create.dir = TRUE ) #> Warning: Removed 342 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). #> Warning: Removed 255 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).  ggsave(   filename = "figures/voles_combined.png",   plot = vole1/vole2,   width = 4,   height = 6.53,   dpi = 600,   create.dir = TRUE ) #> Warning: Removed 1197 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). #> Warning: Removed 1043 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). #> Warning: Removed 342 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). #> Warning: Removed 255 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

Acknowledge requesting gaining permission use Arctos data, agreeing terms Arctos Community Data Policy

title: An Interface to the Arctos Database
code: library(ArctosR)  # You will have to request an API key from Arctos to get records, and pass it # to the `get_record_count` and `get_records` functions through the `api_key` # parameter. YOUR_API_KEY <- "11111111-2222-3333-4444444444444444"  # Request a list of all result parameters. These are the names that can show up # as columns in a dataframe returned by ArctosR. result_params <- get_result_parameters()  # Print the first six rows and first 3 columns to the console. result_params[1:6, 1:3]  # If using RStudio, view the entire dataframe of result parameters. View(result_params)  # Request just the number of records matching a query. count <- get_record_count(   scientific_name = "Canis lupus",    guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",    api_key = YOUR_API_KEY )  # Request to download data. This is limited to 100 records by default. query <- get_records(   scientific_name = "Canis lupus", guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",   columns = list("guid", "parts", "partdetail"),   api_key = YOUR_API_KEY )  # Request to download all available data. query <- get_records(   scientific_name = "Canis lupus", guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",   columns = list("guid", "parts", "partdetail"),   api_key = YOUR_API_KEY,   all_records = TRUE )  # Grab the dataframe of records from the response and save that as a csv. df <- response_data(query)

title: ArctosR: An Interface to the Arctos Database — ArctosR-package
text: ArctosR package provides set functions help users perform requests Arctos API download data. provides set builder classes performing complex requests, well set simple functions automating many common requests workflows.

title: ArctosR: An Interface to the Arctos Database — ArctosR-package
text: Arctos collection management information system serving 5 million records natural cultural history collections. Arctos integrates access collections disciplines anthropology, botany, entomology, ethnology, herpetology, geology, ichthyology, mammalogy, mineralogy, ornithology, paleontology, parasitology, well archival cultural collections. Arctos database accessible web interface https://arctos.database.museum/ information Arctos, can found https://arctosdb.org//, Cicero et al. (2024) doi:10.1371/journal.pone.0296478.

what: Get records from Arctos based on a query — get_records
title: Get records from Arctos based on a query — get_records
text: Make request Arctos return data based query. columns (fields) returned specified list defined columns. list possible query keys can obtained output get_query_parameters.

title: Get records from Arctos based on a query — get_records
code: get_records(..., api_key = NULL, columns = NULL, limit = NULL,             filter_by = NULL, all_records = FALSE)

title: Get records from Arctos based on a query — get_records
text: ... Query parameters values pass Arctos search. example, scientific_name = "Canis lupus" api_key (character) API key use request. columns list columns returned table records downloaded Arctos. limit (numeric) maximum number records download . Default 100. filter_by optional list record attributes filter results . all_records (logical) true, request performed multiple times obtain data Arctos records matching query downloaded.

title: Get records from Arctos based on a query — get_records
text: query object consisting metadata request sent Arctos fulfill user's query, data frame records.

title: Get records from Arctos based on a query — get_records
code: library(ArctosR)  if (interactive()) {   # Request to download all available data   query <- get_records(     scientific_name = "Canis lupus", guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",     columns = list("guid", "parts", "partdetail")   ) }  if (interactive()) {   # Request to download data about rodents examined for Orthohantavirus   orthohantavirus_MSB <- get_records(guid_prefix="MSB:Mamm", taxon_name=Rodentia,                                      filter_by=list("detected"="Orthohantavirus")   ) }

title: Read query records previously saved as an RDS file — read_response_rds
code: library(ArctosR)  if (interactive()) {   # Request to download all available data   query <- get_records(     scientific_name = "Canis lupus", guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",     columns = list("guid", "parts", "partdetail")   )    # Save the data in a .RDS file   save_response_rds(query, "wolves.RDS")    # Load the data from the .RDS just saved   read_response_rds("wolves.RDS")    DONTSHOW({   unlink("wolves.RDS")   }) }

title: save_response_csv — save_response_csv
text: query query object saved filename (character) Name file saved. expanded (logical) Setting option TRUE create folder CSVs representing hierarchical data. See details. with_metadata Whether save metadata response JSON file along side CSV folder CSVs.

title: save_response_csv — save_response_csv
text: Nothing.

title: save_response_csv — save_response_csv
text: columns Arctos tables, accurately represent structure data, inner tables can saved separate CSVs named according record belong.

title: save_response_csv — save_response_csv
code: library(ArctosR)  if (interactive()) {   # Request to download all available data   query <- get_records(     scientific_name = "Canis lupus", guid_prefix = "MSB:Mamm",     columns = list("guid", "parts", "partdetail")   )    # Save the response in a flat CSV with an additional metadata file in JSON   save_response_csv(query, "msb-wolves.csv", with_metadata = TRUE)    DONTSHOW({   unlink("msb-wolves.csv")   unlink("msb-wolves.csv.json")   }) }

title: ArctosR 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-11 Initial CRAN submission.

title: ArctosR 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2025-07-15 Fix failing test macOS Introduce functions assist troubleshooting failed requests

title: ArctosR 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-15 Package longer package-level API key. Users need request API key ‘Arctos’ Improve error reporting functions Add get_relationships function

title: ArctosR 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2025-09-23 Fix get_records subsequent requests using user’s API key Add get_request_url get first URL used query Update vignette demonstrate spatial data plotting
